title: HTML Image Extractor
shortDescription: Extract image sources, alt text, and dimensions from HTML.
metaTitle: HTML Image Extractor | Extract Image URLs, Alt Text and Dimensions
metaDescription: Extract image sources, alt text, width and height from HTML instantly in your browser. Safe, client-side and easy to use.
intro: Use this HTML image extractor to quickly pull image URLs, alt text, and dimensions from any HTML code. It runs in your browser, works client-side only, and helps you review image tags without external APIs.
overview: Paste HTML and extract all image tags into a clean, readable list. The tool can show src, alt, width, height, and other useful attributes so you can audit content, check accessibility, and reuse image data faster. Because processing happens locally, it is a safe option for working with sensitive markup.
howItWorks: Paste your HTML into the input field.
The tool scans the markup for img elements.
It extracts image source URLs and common attributes such as alt, width, and height.
Review the structured output and copy the results if needed.
useCases: Audit image accessibility by checking alt text coverage.
Collect image URLs from exported HTML.
Review width and height values before publishing.
Extract image references from CMS or email templates.
Speed up QA for pages with many image tags.

examples.title: Basic image tag
examples.output: src: /images/banner.jpg | alt: Homepage banner | width: 1200 | height: 400
examples.description: A single image tag is parsed into structured fields.

examples.title: Lazy-loaded image
examples.description: Useful for checking alternate image source attributes in templates.

faq.question: Does this tool upload my HTML?
faq.answer: No. It works entirely in your browser, so your HTML is not sent to a server.

faq.question: Which image attributes are extracted?
faq.answer: The tool focuses on src, alt, width, and height, and may also surface other relevant image attributes when available.

faq.question: Can it extract images from unsafe HTML?
faq.answer: Yes, it can parse HTML safely on the client side, but you should still review any untrusted markup carefully.

faq.question: Does it detect CSS background images?
faq.answer: No. It extracts image tags from HTML, not background images from CSS.

seo.title: HTML Image Extractor
seo.description: Extract image sources, alt text, width and height from HTML with a safe client-side tool.
